title: A Lost World of Ancient Animals Found in China
summary: Scientists have discovered a special fossil site in China that shows animals were more complex earlier than we thought. They found fossils from over 539 million years ago, before a time called the Cambrian Period when many animals suddenly appeared. This new site, called the Jiangchuan Biota, has fossils of early relatives of starfish, worms, and even animals like humans. The discovery helps us understand how animal life started on Earth, showing that many groups were already around earlier than scientists believed. It's like finding a lost world that changes our ideas about evolution.

background_read: This article is about a big discovery in science. Scientists found old fossils in China that show animals were more advanced earlier than we knew. Before this, people thought a time called the Cambrian Period was when many animals first appeared. But these fossils are from before that time, called the Ediacaran period. They include early relatives of animals like starfish and worms. This helps scientists learn how life on Earth changed over millions of years. It's important because it fills in missing pieces of the story of evolution.
Article_Structure: WHO: Scientists from Oxford University and Yunnan University
WHAT: Found fossils showing early complex animals before the Cambrian Period
WHERE: In southwest China at the Jiangchuan Biota site
WHY: To understand how animal life first developed on Earth

perspectives.description: This shows that animal evolution started earlier than previously thought, changing our timeline of life on Earth.
